# Unpushed changes review

## Overall verdict

**Direction is correct.** The branch does the right structural work:

- Deletes duplicate SQL modules under [`relocation_jobs/db/`](relocation_jobs/db/) and merges catalog [`writes.py`](relocation_jobs/catalog/writes.py) / [`stats.py`](relocation_jobs/catalog/stats.py) into [`catalog/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py)
- Splits oversized [`fetch/runner.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/runner.py) and [`panel/flatten.py`](relocation_jobs/panel/flatten.py)
- Adds EC2 fetch worker ([`Dockerfile.ec2-worker`](Dockerfile.ec2-worker), [`scripts/fetch_scheduler_worker.py`](scripts/fetch_scheduler_worker.py), [`fetch/scheduler.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/scheduler.py))
- Moves admin scraping to read-only worker observability ([`admin-worker.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin-worker.js))
- Fixes board-load perf path ([`flatten_companies_for_stats`](relocation_jobs/panel/service.py), lighter `load_context` flags)

**Not push-ready yet** because of: (1) a failing test contradiction, (2) unfinished `db/__init__.py` facade, (3) several rule violations carried into merged files, (4) one admin UX regression.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph before [Before]
    dbUsers[db/users.py]
    dbTrack[db/tracking.py]
    catalogWrites[catalog/writes.py]
    bigRunner[fetch/runner.py]
    bigFlatten[panel/flatten.py]
  end
  subgraph after [After - target]
    usersRepo[users/repo.py]
    positionsRepo[positions/repo.py]
    catalogRepo[catalog/repo.py]
    fetchState[fetch/state.py]
    flattenRules[panel/flatten_rules.py]
  end
  dbUsers --> usersRepo
  dbTrack --> positionsRepo
  catalogWrites --> catalogRepo
  bigRunner --> fetchState
  bigFlatten --> flattenRules
```

---

## P0 — Fix before push (blocking)

| File | Change | Why |
|------|--------|-----|
| [`tests/web/test_catalog_api.py`](tests/web/test_catalog_api.py) | `assert payload["panel_stats"] is None` (not `is not None`) | Contradicts [`get_admin_dashboard`](relocation_jobs/admin/service.py) and [`test_admin_e2e.py`](tests/web/test_admin_e2e.py); one test will fail |
| [`tests/panel/test_preview_board.py`](tests/panel/test_preview_board.py) | Add to git (currently untracked) | Covers `preview_board_company` behavior referenced in [`board-load-performance-incident.md`](docs/reference/board-load-performance-incident.md) |
| [`relocation_jobs/static/js/admin-worker.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin-worker.js) | Set `fetchBusy = true` on "View live progress"; restore cancel fallback via `getFetchStatus()` when `fetchBusy` is false | Cancel button is dead on manual progress view — regression vs deleted [`admin-scrape.js`](relocation_jobs/static/js/admin-scrape.js) |

---

## P1 — Finish consolidation (engineering-standards §1, §5; rules.md "no facades")

The duplicate SQL files were deleted correctly, but the migration stopped halfway.

| File | Change | Why |
|------|--------|-----|
| [`relocation_jobs/db/__init__.py`](relocation_jobs/db/__init__.py) | Shrink to bootstrap-only: `init_db`, `get_connection`, `db_transaction`, `reset_db_initialized`. Remove ~40 re-exports, `*_db` aliases, `fetch_status_from_row` / `idle_fetch_status` (duplicate of [`fetch/state.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/state.py)), `tracking_is_empty` (SQL outside repo, **zero callers**), `record_fetch_run` stub | Violates **"No re-export facades"** and **"SQL only in repo"**; main remaining structural debt from this branch |
| [`relocation_jobs/core/auth.py`](relocation_jobs/core/auth.py) | Import `create_user`, `get_user_by_*`, `is_user_admin`, `update_user_password`, `user_count` from `users.repo`; move import block to top of file | Still routes through forbidden facade; imports currently appear after `_hash_password` |
| [`relocation_jobs/admin/service.py`](relocation_jobs/admin/service.py) | `list_users_with_stats`, `user_count` → `users.repo` | Same facade violation |
| [`relocation_jobs/web/routes/admin.py`](relocation_jobs/web/routes/admin.py) | `list_users_with_stats` → `users.repo` | Same |
| [`relocation_jobs/web/routes/fetch.py`](relocation_jobs/web/routes/fetch.py) | `is_user_admin` → `users.repo` | Same |
| [`tests/conftest.py`](tests/conftest.py), [`tests/web/test_*.py`](tests/web/) | `get_user_by_username` / `create_user` → `users.repo`; keep `init_db` via bootstrap path only | Tests still reinforce the facade |

---

## P2 — Rule violations in merged / new modules

### Catalog ([`catalog/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py) — 1,474 lines)

Consolidation is architecturally right; the merged file is not yet rule-compliant.

| Issue | Files to change | Why |

| 9 functions >40 lines (`get_catalog_overview` 135, `upsert_company_and_jobs` 133, …) | `catalog/repo.py` | Known violations backlog; worsened by merge |
| Docstrings on write functions (migrated from deleted `writes.py`) | `catalog/repo.py` | rules.md: no docstrings by default |

| Duplicate upsert paths (`sync_company_board_to_catalog` vs `upsert_company_and_jobs` / `save_country_catalog`) | `catalog/repo.py` | Fragility risk; same job-sync logic in two shapes |
| Lazy import inside `invalidate_country_cache` | [`catalog/cache.py`](relocation_jobs/catalog/cache.py) | **New** violation introduced by this branch; move `invalidate_country_labels_cache` to module top |
| Module docstring on `cache.py` | `catalog/cache.py` | Style drift vs v2 default |

Optional later: move `get_catalog_overview` (pure aggregation, no SQL) to a `catalog/service.py` if you want strict repo = persistence only.

### Fetch / panel refactors

| File | Change | Why |
|------|--------|-----|
| [`fetch/runner.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/runner.py) | Remove assignment re-exports (`build_fetch_status = fetch_state...`); update [`web/routes/admin.py`](relocation_jobs/web/routes/admin.py) and tests to import from `fetch.state` | Forbidden re-export facade |
| [`fetch/runner.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/runner.py) | Use `make_fetch_client()` in `run_single_company_fetch_async` instead of bare `httpx.AsyncClient()` | Consistency with extracted [`fetch/client.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/client.py) |
| [`panel/flatten.py`](relocation_jobs/panel/flatten.py) | Remove `_skip_company_after_jobs` alias; remove docstring on `preview_board_company` | Facade alias + docstring violation |
| [`tests/panel/test_hide_empty.py`](tests/panel/test_hide_empty.py) | Import `skip_company_after_jobs` from `flatten_rules` | Stop depending on private alias |
| [`fetch/state.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/state.py), [`fetch/country_runner.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/country_runner.py) | Remove docstrings on `guard_fetch_start`, `_fetch_one_thread` | rules.md |
| [`fetch/pipeline.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/pipeline.py) | Drop unused `CatalogPersister` import | Dead import |
| [`fetch/pipeline.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/pipeline.py) + [`fetch/service.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/service.py) + [`scrape/company.py`](relocation_jobs/scrape/company.py) | Rename `persist_board` → explicit sync name at scrape/fetch boundary | Known violation #3 in rules.md; unchanged by this branch |

**Long functions still over target** (acceptable backlog, but note): `country_runner.run_country_fetch` (~145), `scheduler.run_fetch_cycle` (~75), `flatten_jobs.partition_stored_jobs` (~80), `state.reset_for_run` / `persist_fetch_run`.

---

## P3 — Admin layer cleanup (dead code + DRY)

| File | Change | Why |
|------|--------|-----|
| [`admin/service.py`](relocation_jobs/admin/service.py) | Wire `compute_admin_panel_stats` into route **or** delete it; remove dead `user_id` / `timezone_name` params from `get_admin_dashboard` (`del` on entry) | Function defined but never called; route duplicates same logic inline — violates route thinness |
| [`web/routes/admin.py`](relocation_jobs/web/routes/admin.py) | Delegate `api_admin_panel_stats` to `admin_service.compute_admin_panel_stats` | Layer boundary: routes should not own flatten+stats orchestration |
| [`admin/service.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/scheduler.py) | Extract shared schedule env parsing (`_schedule_interval_hours` duplicated) | DRY / fragility |

|------|--------|-----|

[`docs/operations/ec2-panel.md`](docs/operations/ec2-panel.md) and [`docs/README.md`](docs/README.md) look aligned with deploy changes.

| File | Change | Why |
|------|--------|-----|
| [`scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh`](scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh) | Pass `REDIS_URL` to worker container (panel already has it) | Worker may diverge on custom country labels when Redis is enabled |
| [`scripts/fetch_scheduler_worker.py`](scripts/fetch_scheduler_worker.py) | Move imports to top (optional) | Minor import-discipline drift; bootstrap scripts are gray area |

---

## Files that look good — no change required

These align with skills/docs principles:

- **Deleted dead modules:** `db/companies.py`, `db/events.py`, `db/fetch_runs.py`, `db/tracking.py`, `db/users.py`, `catalog/writes.py`, `catalog/stats.py`, `positions/tracking_resolve.py`, `static/js/admin-scrape.js`
- **SQL moved to repos:** tracking writes → [`positions/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/positions/repo.py); user/event reads → [`users/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/users/repo.py); fetch runs → [`fetch/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/repo.py)
- **Lazy import fixes:** `httpx` at top of `fetch/runner.py`; `_make_fetch_client` no longer imported inside `country_runner`
- **Panel perf:** [`flatten_companies_for_stats`](relocation_jobs/panel/service.py), [`load_context` flags](relocation_jobs/panel/service.py), [`location_tags` caching](relocation_jobs/core/location_tags.py)
- **Flatten decomposition:** [`flatten_rules.py`](relocation_jobs/panel/flatten_rules.py) follows types → rules → API well
- **Scheduler tests:** [`tests/fetch/test_scheduler.py`](tests/fetch/test_scheduler.py)
- **Deploy split:** panel `PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=0`, worker `=1` in [`ec2_app_deploy.sh`](scripts/ec2_app_deploy.sh)
- **MCP migration:** [`mcp/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/mcp/repo.py), [`mcp/service.py`](relocation_jobs/mcp/service.py) off `db.users`

---

## Suggested fix order

1. **P0** — test conflict, untracked test, admin-worker cancel bug
2. **P1** — finish `db/__init__.py` shrink + repoint ~8 callers to `users.repo`
3. **P3** — admin service/route DRY + dead code
4. **P2** — facade re-exports in `fetch/runner.py` and `panel/flatten.py`; `catalog/cache.py` lazy import; delete `touch_country_meta`
5. **P4** — docs sync
6. **P2 backlog** — `catalog/repo.py` section order + long functions + naming (`save_country_catalog`); `persist_board` rename across scrape stack
7. **P5** — worker Redis parity if you use custom countries in prod

---

## Intentional asymmetry (no change unless you want it)

Tracking **reads** in `users/repo.py` vs **writes** in `positions/repo.py` is asymmetric but workable for panel context loading. Not a rules violation — just be deliberate if you ever split a `tracking/` domain.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Reset a panel user's password (e.g. after changing .env)."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import os
import sys

from relocation_jobs.core.auth import _hash_password
from relocation_jobs.core.paths import PROJECT_ROOT
from relocation_jobs.db import init_db
from relocation_jobs.users.repo import (
    get_user_by_username,
    rename_user,
    update_user_password,
)


def _load_env() -> None:
    try:
        from dotenv import load_dotenv
        load_dotenv(PROJECT_ROOT / ".env")
    except ImportError:
        pass


def main() -> int:
    _load_env()
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Reset panel login password")
    parser.add_argument(
        "username",
        nargs="?",
        default=os.environ.get("PANEL_ADMIN_USER", "admin"),
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "password",
        nargs="?",
        default=os.environ.get("PANEL_ADMIN_PASSWORD", ""),
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--rename-from",
        help="Rename this existing username to the target username first",
    )
    args = parser.parse_args()

    if not args.password:
        print("Password required (argument or PANEL_ADMIN_PASSWORD in .env)", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    init_db()
    username = args.username.strip()
    if args.rename_from:
        old = get_user_by_username(args.rename_from)
        if not old:
            print(f"User not found: {args.rename_from}", file=sys.stderr)
            return 1
        if get_user_by_username(username) and old["username"].lower() != username.lower():
            print(f"Username already taken: {username}", file=sys.stderr)
            return 1
        rename_user(old["id"], username)
        print(f"Renamed {args.rename_from} → {username}")

    if not update_user_password(username, _hash_password(args.password)):
        print(f"User not found: {username}", file=sys.stderr)
        return 1

    print(f"Password updated for {username}")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

    return {
        row["country"]: {

            "stored_visa_jobs": int(row.get("visa_jobs") or 0),

    }


def _overview_country_rows(
    company_rows: list[dict],
    jobs_by_country: dict[str, dict],
    empty_companies: int,
) -> tuple[list[dict], dict]:
    countries: list[dict] = []
    totals = {
        "companies": 0,
        "jobs": 0,
        "stored_jobs": 0,
        "visa_jobs": 0,
        "stored_visa_jobs": 0,
        "fetch_problems": 0,
        "fetch_ok": 0,
        "empty_companies": empty_companies,
        "missing_locations": 0,
    }
    for row in company_rows:
        country = row["country"]
        stored = jobs_by_country.get(country, {"stored_jobs": 0, "stored_visa_jobs": 0})
        companies_count = int(row.get("companies") or 0)
        stored_jobs = int(stored.get("stored_jobs") or 0)
        stored_visa = int(stored.get("stored_visa_jobs") or 0)
        countries.append({
            "country": country,
            "label": country_label(country),
            "companies": companies_count,
            "jobs": stored_jobs,
            "visa_jobs": stored_visa,
            "fetch_problems": int(row.get("fetch_problems") or 0),
            "fetch_ok": int(row.get("fetch_ok") or 0),
            "missing_locations": int(row.get("missing_locations") or 0),
        })
        totals["companies"] += companies_count
        totals["stored_jobs"] += stored_jobs
        totals["stored_visa_jobs"] += stored_visa
        totals["jobs"] += stored_jobs
        totals["visa_jobs"] += stored_visa
        totals["fetch_problems"] += int(row.get("fetch_problems") or 0)
        totals["fetch_ok"] += int(row.get("fetch_ok") or 0)
        totals["missing_locations"] += int(row.get("missing_locations") or 0)
    countries.sort(key=lambda row: row["country"])
    return countries, totals


def _overview_country_meta(
    meta_rows: list[dict],

    countries: list[dict],
) -> list[dict]:
    companies_by_country = {c["country"]: c["companies"] for c in countries}
    meta_by_country: dict[str, dict] = {}
    for row in meta_rows:
        country = row.get("country", "")
        last_fetch = _max_timestamp(
            row.get("jobs_fetched"),
            latest_job_by_country.get(country),
        )
        meta_by_country[country] = {
            "country": country,
            "label": country_label(country or ""),
            "source": row.get("source"),
            "catalog_imported": row.get("fetched"),
            "last_fetch": last_fetch,
            "updated": row.get("updated"),
            "jobs_fetched": row.get("jobs_fetched"),
            "total": companies_by_country.get(country, int(row.get("total") or 0)),
            "last_fetch_new_jobs": int(row.get("last_fetch_new_jobs") or 0),
        }

    for country_row in countries:
        country = country_row["country"]

            "updated": "",
            "jobs_fetched": "",
            "total": country_row["companies"],
            "last_fetch_new_jobs": 0,
        }
    return sorted(meta_by_country.values(), key=lambda row: row["country"])

            "fetch_ok": int(row.get("fetch_ok") or 0),
            "missing_locations": int(row.get("missing_locations") or 0),
        })
        totals["companies"] += companies_count
        totals["stored_jobs"] += stored_jobs
        totals["stored_visa_jobs"] += stored_visa
        totals["jobs"] += stored_jobs
        totals["visa_jobs"] += stored_visa
        totals["fetch_problems"] += int(row.get("fetch_problems") or 0)
        totals["fetch_ok"] += int(row.get("fetch_ok") or 0)
        totals["missing_locations"] += int(row.get("missing_locations") or 0)

    countries.sort(key=lambda row: row["country"])
    by_ats = [
        {"ats_type": row["ats_type"], "companies": int(row.get("companies") or 0)}
        for row in ats_rows
    ]
    fetch_problem_companies = [
        {
            "country": row.get("country"),
            "name": row.get("name"),
            "fetch_problem_date": row.get("fetch_problem_date"),
            "careers_url": row.get("careers_url"),
            "ats_type": row.get("ats_type"),
        }
        for row in problem_rows
    ]

    companies_by_country = {c["country"]: c["companies"] for c in countries}
    meta_by_country: dict[str, dict] = {}
    for row in meta_rows:
        country = row.get("country", "")
        last_fetch = _max_timestamp(
            row.get("jobs_fetched"),
            latest_job_by_country.get(country),
        )
        meta_by_country[country] = {
            "country": country,
            "label": country_label(country or ""),
            "source": row.get("source"),
            "catalog_imported": row.get("fetched"),
            "last_fetch": last_fetch,
            "updated": row.get("updated"),
            "jobs_fetched": row.get("jobs_fetched"),
            "total": companies_by_country.get(country, int(row.get("total") or 0)),
            "last_fetch_new_jobs": int(row.get("last_fetch_new_jobs") or 0),
        }

    for country_row in countries:
        country = country_row["country"]
        if country in meta_by_country:
            continue
        meta_by_country[country] = {
            "country": country,
            "label": country_row["label"],
            "source": "",
            "catalog_imported": "",
            "last_fetch": latest_job_by_country.get(country, ""),
            "updated": "",
            "jobs_fetched": "",
            "total": country_row["companies"],
            "last_fetch_new_jobs": 0,
        }

    return {
        "has_data": True,
        "countries": countries,
        "totals": totals,
        "by_ats": by_ats,
        "fetch_problem_companies": fetch_problem_companies,
        "country_meta": sorted(meta_by_country.values(), key=lambda row: row["country"]),
    }

def _upsert_company_row_on_conn(
    conn,
    country_key: str,
    company: dict,
    *,
    updated: str,
) -> int:
    name = (company.get("name") or "").strip()
    added = company.get("added") or updated
    company_updated = company.get("updated") or updated
    sync_company_location_fields(company, catalog_country=country_key)
    params = (
        country_key,
        name,
        company.get("city") or "",
        cities_json_from_company(company),
        locations_json_from_company(company, catalog_country=country_key),
        company.get("size") or "",
        company.get("careers_url") or "",
        company.get("ats_type") or "",
        company.get("ats_url") or "",
        1 if company.get("fetch_problem") else 0,
        company.get("fetch_problem_date"),
        1 if company.get("fetch_ok") else 0,
        company.get("fetch_ok_date"),
        added,
        company_updated,
        json_sources(company),
    )
    cur = conn.execute(
        """
        INSERT INTO companies (
            country, name, city, cities_json, locations_json, size, careers_url, ats_type, ats_url,
            fetch_problem, fetch_problem_date, fetch_ok, fetch_ok_date,
            added, updated, sources_json
        ) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
        ON CONFLICT (country, name) DO UPDATE SET
            city = EXCLUDED.city,
            cities_json = EXCLUDED.cities_json,
            locations_json = EXCLUDED.locations_json,
            size = EXCLUDED.size,
            careers_url = EXCLUDED.careers_url,
            ats_type = EXCLUDED.ats_type,
            ats_url = EXCLUDED.ats_url,
            fetch_problem = EXCLUDED.fetch_problem,
            fetch_problem_date = EXCLUDED.fetch_problem_date,
            fetch_ok = EXCLUDED.fetch_ok,
            fetch_ok_date = EXCLUDED.fetch_ok_date,
            updated = EXCLUDED.updated,
            sources_json = EXCLUDED.sources_json
        RETURNING id
        """,
        params,
    )
    row = cur.fetchone()
    if row is None:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to upsert company {name!r}")
    return row["id"] if isinstance(row, dict) else row[0]


def _merge_matching_jobs_on_conn(conn, company_id: int, jobs: list[dict]) -> None:
    keys: list[str] = []
    job_rows: list[tuple] = []
    for job in jobs:
        stamp_job_identity(job)
        key = job.get("idempotency_key") or job_idempotency_key(job.get("url", ""))
        if not key:
            continue
        keys.append(key)
        job_rows.append((
            company_id,
            key,
            job.get("title") or "",
            job.get("url") or "",
            job.get("fetched") or "",
            job.get("last_seen") or job.get("fetched") or "",
            _visa_to_db(job.get("visa_sponsorship")),
            (job.get("location") or "").strip(),
            job_locations_json(job),
            (job.get("description_text") or "").strip(),
        ))

    for row in job_rows:
        conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO matching_jobs (
                company_id, idempotency_key, title, url, fetched, last_seen,
                visa_sponsorship, location, locations_json, description_text
            ) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
            ON CONFLICT (company_id, idempotency_key) DO UPDATE SET
                title = EXCLUDED.title,
                url = EXCLUDED.url,
                fetched = EXCLUDED.fetched,
                last_seen = EXCLUDED.last_seen,
                visa_sponsorship = EXCLUDED.visa_sponsorship,
                location = COALESCE(NULLIF(EXCLUDED.location, ''), matching_jobs.location),
                locations_json = CASE
                    WHEN EXCLUDED.locations_json IS NOT NULL
                         AND EXCLUDED.locations_json != '[]'
                    THEN EXCLUDED.locations_json
                    ELSE matching_jobs.locations_json
                END,
                description_text = CASE
                    WHEN EXCLUDED.description_text IS NOT NULL
                         AND EXCLUDED.description_text != ''
                    THEN EXCLUDED.description_text
                    ELSE matching_jobs.description_text
                END
            """,
            row,
        )

    if keys:
        placeholders = ", ".join(["%s"] * len(keys))
        conn.execute(
            f"""
            DELETE FROM matching_jobs
            WHERE company_id = %s AND idempotency_key NOT IN ({placeholders})
            """,
            (company_id, *keys),
        )
    else:
        conn.execute(
            "DELETE FROM matching_jobs WHERE company_id = %s",
            (company_id,),
        )

    conn,

    _merge_matching_jobs_on_conn(conn, company_id, company.get("matching_jobs") or [])

content.text:
**Mostly yes for a branch push — not quite “ship to prod and walk away” without a quick smoke check.**

## What gives me confidence

- **270 / 271 tests pass** in the main v2 suite. The one failure (`test_detect_ats_static_finds_workday_for_criteo`) looks unrelated to this refactor — ATS detection, not db/fetch/panel/admin.
- **Plan items P0–P4 and the backlog renames are done:** `db/__init__.py` is bootstrap-only, facade re-exports removed, `sync_country_catalog` / `sync_board` naming, admin route DRY, docs updated.
- **Architecture direction is sound:** duplicate SQL modules deleted, logic lives in domain repos, fetch worker split is coherent.
- **Targeted areas have coverage:** scheduler tests, admin e2e, preview board, catalog repo, fetch service.

## What I’d still treat as risk

| Risk | Severity | Notes |
|------|----------|--------|
| **Large blast radius** | Medium | ~80 files, deletes old `db/*` and `catalog/writes.py`. Any missed import path breaks at runtime, not in tests. |
| **Worker has no `REDIS_URL`** | Low–medium | P5 wasn’t done. Panel gets Redis; worker doesn’t. Fine if you don’t use custom countries via Redis; otherwise worker and panel can disagree on country labels. |
| **No deploy smoke test** | Medium | EC2 worker + panel split wasn’t exercised end-to-end here. Worth `deploy` + check admin worker card + one manual fetch cancel from “View live progress”. |
| **`catalog/repo.py` still ~1,470 lines** | Low | Improved (helpers split, docstrings removed), but not fully reordered. Maintainability debt, not a push blocker. |
| **Untracked docs** | Low | `board-load-performance-incident.md` is referenced by tests/code; consider adding it. `kafka-fetch-pipeline-proposal.md` is separate. |
| **Scrape board tests not run** | Low | Async board tests were skipped in this environment; unrelated modules but worth CI. |

## Verdict

**Push to a branch:** yes, with good trust — the refactor is internally consistent and tests back the main paths.

**Merge / deploy to kuchup.com:** I’d do one short checklist first:

1. Run full `pytest tests -o addopts=` in your normal venv (with asyncio/respx).
2. Smoke admin: dashboard loads, `panel_stats` loads async, worker status shows, cancel works from progress modal.
3. If you use Redis custom countries: add `REDIS_URL` to the worker container in `ec2_app_deploy.sh`, or accept the divergence.
4. Stage `tests/panel/test_preview_board.py` and any docs you want in the commit (e.g. `board-load-performance-incident.md`).

**Bottom line:** This is no longer “don’t push” territory. It’s “push with confidence, then verify deploy behavior once” — especially the new worker container and admin observability path, which tests don’t fully cover.
